Fact.Name: Overtime Compensation Excluding Qualified Tip Overtime Comp
Fact.Description: OB3 §70202 IRC §225(c)(2). Overtime compensation excluding all qualified tips defined in IRC §224 that were received as part of qualified overtime compensation

Fact.Name: Maximum overtime compensation deduction for non MFJ filers
Maximum overtime compensation deduction for MFJ filers
Has the taxpayer received overtime comp this tax years?
Fact.Description: OB3 Section 70202 temporarily creating §225(c)(1): overtime comp received after 1/1/25 that is "that is in excess of the regular rate (as used in such section) at which such individual is employed."

Fact.Name: Is the taxpayer non-exempt and eligible for qualified overtime comp under FLSA Section 7?
Fact.Description: OB3 Section 70202 temporarily creating §225(c)(1): For purposes of this section, the term ‘qualified overtime compensation’ means overtime compensation paid to an individual required under section 7 of the Fair Labor Standards Act of 1938

Fact.Name: Taxpayer eligible for overtime compensation deduction
Fact.Description: Is the taxpayer eligible for the overtime compensation deduction?

Fact.Name: Qualified Overtime Deduction Phase Out
Fact.Description: The amount allowable as a deduction under shall be reduced (but not below zero) by $100 for each $1,000 by which the taxpayer’s MAGI exceeds $150,000 ($300,000 in the case of a joint return).

Fact.Name: Overtime Compensation Deduction
Fact.Description: OB3 Section 70202: Qualified tip deductions Example: Single filer with 160k MAGI and 10k overtime compensations. 150k applicable overtime compensation threshold (20,000 excess/1000)*100 = 2,000 phase out 10,000 overtime comp - 2,000 phase out = 8,000 applicable deduction

Form 8959 Part I Line 1. Per the Form instructions, this should include the total Medicare wages and tips plus your self-employment income, if any (including the Medicare wages and tips and self-employment income of your spouse, if married filing jointly), are greater than the threshold amount for your filing status. See example 5 https://www.irs.gov/instructions/i8959
Form 8959 Part I Line 2
Form 8959 Part I Line 3
Form 8959 Part I Line 4
Form 8959 Part I Line 5 and II Line 9
Form 8959 Part I Line 6
Form 8959 Part I Line 7

Form 8959 Part I Line 8. Per the Form instructions, this should include the total Medicare wages and tips plus your self-employment income, if any (including the Medicare wages and tips and self-employment income of your spouse, if married filing jointly), are greater than the threshold amount for your filing status
Form8959 Line 17
The tentative number of remaining pay periods, for a job that will be held later in the year.
A preliminary calculation of remaining pay periods for weekly and biweekly frequencies, to be adjusted because it may extend past the end of the year.
For weekly and biweekly jobs, the final pay date predicted by the tentative calculation of remaining pay periods-- which may be past the end of year.
mostRecentPayPeriodEnd is on the last day of the month
Has lagging payments from the prior year
For semimonthly pay periods, determines the earlier pay period ending date
For semimonthly pay periods, determines the later pay period ending date
The month in which the user last worked as far as income counting towards this year is concerned, used for semi-monthly and monthly
The date that is effectively the user's last day worked for this job as far as income counting towards this year is concerned
similar to jobs/*/effectiveEndDate but only up to the last full pay period
Similar to jobs/*/ordinalEffectiveEndDate but only until the end of the last full payment
Is paid for a partial partial period
# of full pay periods that are left to be paid this year

# of full pay periods that are left to be paid this year, including as a decimal the last, partial pay period (if there is one).
For instance, if the taxpayer has three weeks of work left, and they are paid biweekly:
/jobs/*/fullPayPeriods = 1 /jobs/*/remainingPayPeriodsRational = 1.5 /jobs/*/remainingPayPeriodsInteger = 2
Rationale representing the fractional pay period that is paid
Calculated as a combined number/decimal value.
The fractional amount of remaining pay periods, for use in calculating resulting eoy total income.

The number of pay periods remaining in the year. If the taxpayer is only working for part of their final pay period, that pay period is included
Monthly and semimonthly are computed directly.
Weekly and biweekly are computed using `../tentativeRemainingPayPeriods` and `../predictedOrdinalFinalPayDate` to make any necessary adjustment when the tentative calculation overflows the end of year.
For example, let's say you are paid weekly every Friday for the previous week, and you're working through EOY. Then (in 2025) you will be paid on the 2nd of January 2026 for 12/21-27, and on the 9th of January 2026 for 12/28-01/03. That's two pay periods that are in 2025 that will be paid for (and taxed in) 2026.

If you're a K-12 educator, you can deduct up to $300 of unreimbursed expenses. If you're filing a joint tax return and both of you are educators, you can deduct up to $600, but each individual cannot deduct more than $300 per person. Qualified expenses include: Professional development courses Books, supplies, computer equipment (including related software and services) Other equipment, and supplementary materials that you use in the classroom
Lesser of the amount input by the taxpayer and the filing status limit

Fact.Name: Maximum Deduction for educator expenses
Fact.Description: The maximum deduction an individual can take for educator expense
Whether this pension is attributed to the primary filer or spouse if MFJ
Total non-job or pension/annuity income.
Other deductions to income.
Projected yearly pay with deductions removed.
Adjusted total other income (2c), or adjusted annual payment amount (2d).

Standard withholding rate schedules for the form W-4P. Line 2j of Pub. 15-T Worksheet 1B. These amounts are generally a base withholding amount plus a percentage of the excess of Annual Adjusted Wages Amount. The base withholding captures the total withholding for all the brackets below the one that the AAWA falls into.
Since we don't enter Part II of Step 2, this is effectively also representing Line 2u of Pub. 15-T Worksheet 1B.
The tentative annual withholding amount. Pub. 15T Worksheet 1B Line 2u.
Line 4c of Pub. 15-T Worksheet 1B. The per-period withholding amount sub credits, excluding any extra elected on Form W-4P Line 4(c).
The start date of the pension this year
The end date of the pension this year
Average withholding from a specific pension per pay-period.
Length of pay periods matching the Floor of Publication 15-T values.
This pension's withholding that cannot chnage because it's past or within the time lag before W4 Changes kick in.
The number of pay dates remaining for this pension that will be paid out within the calendar year.
Logic dictating which pension will be assigned additional or reduced withholding. If there are any jobs, then those will be assigned the withholding modifications and pensions will remain untouched.
Whether this is attributed to the primary filer or spouse if MFJ
The income doesn't belong to the spouse
Options for social security federal taxes withheld
The rate at which taxes are withheld
The rate at which taxes are withheld as a rationale
If the social secuirty source has already ended or ended today
Whether the social security income is still continues into the future or we don't know yet
Gross SSA Income from forms SSA-1099 and RRB-1099
Total amount from box 5 of ALL your Forms SSA-1099 and RRB-1099. Form 1040 line 6a. Pub 915 Worksheet 1, Line 1.
Pub 915 Worksheet 1, Line 2.

Pub 915 Worksheet 1, Line 3. Combine the amounts from Form 1040 or 1040 SR lines 1z, 2b, 3b, 4b, 5b, 7, and 8'. This includes job wages, tips, Medicaid waiver payments, taxable dependent care benefits, employer-provided adoption benefits, taxable interest (2b), ordinary dividends (3b), IRA distributions taxable amounts (4b), pensions and annuities taxable amount (5b), capital gain or loss (7), additional income from Schedule 1 line 10 (8)
Pub 915 Worksheet 1, Line 4. Enter the amount from Form 1040 or Form 1040-SR 2a. Non-taxable interest income, such as interest on municipal bonds

Pub 915 Worksheet 1, Line 5. Enter the total of any exclusions/adjustments for: • Adoption benefits (Form 8839, line 30), • Foreign earned income or housing (Form 2555, lines 45 and 50), and • Certain income of bona fide residents of American Samoa (Form 4563, line 15) or Puerto Rico
Pub 915 Worksheet 1, Line 6.
Sum of adjustments from Form 1044 Schedule 1. Student loan interest deduction should not be included. IRS Pub 915 Worksheet 1, Line 7.
Modifed AGI used to determine taxable amount of SSA benefits. IRS Pub 915 Worksheet 1, Line 8.

Threshold when taxable portion of SSA Benefits changes from 0% to 50%. Pub 915 Worksheet 1, Line 9. If SSA MAGI (Line 8) is less than $32K for MFJ and $25K for anybody else, 0% of SSA benefits are taxed. This is our bracket zero. Taxable portion of SSA MAGI which is more than bracket zero but less than $32K + $12K for MFJ and $25K + $9K for evereryone else, is 50%, not to exceed 50% of SSA Benefits total. Taxable portion of SSA MAGI which is more than $32K + $12K for MFJ and $25K + $9K for evereryone else, is 85%, not to exceed 85% of SSA Benefits total. If TP is MFS which lived together for any part of the year, all SSA Benefits are taxed at 85%.
MAGI which exceeds zero threshold. Pub 915 Worksheet 1, Line 10
Income bracket where taxable portion is 50%. Pub 915 Worksheet 1, Line 11
Amount of MAGI with taxable portion 50%. Pub 915 Worksheet 1, Line 13
Taxable amount for 50% bracket. Should not exceed 50% of total SSA Benefits. Pub 915 Worksheet 1, Line 15
Taxable amount for 85% bracket. Pub 915 Worksheet 1, Line 16
Taxable amount of Social Security benefits to enter in Form 1040 line 6b. Ranges from 0% to up to 50% to up to 85% of total SSA benefits
Choose correct upper income limit for 0% calculations based on filing status. Line 6 - Qualified Dividends and Capital Gain Worksheet
Choose correct upper income limit for 15% calculations based on filing status. Line 13 - Qualified Dividends and Capital Gain Worksheet
The sum of Qualified Dividends and Long-Term Capital Gains. Line 4 - Qualified Dividends and Capital Gain Worksheet
The smaller value of taxable income vs tier 1 upper limit. Line 7 - Qualified Dividends and Capital Gain Worksheet
The smaller value of taxable income vs [taxable income minus qualified dividends]. Line 8 - Qualified Dividends and Capital Gain Worksheet
Amount of Qualified Dividends Taxed at 0%. Line 9 and Line 11 - Qualified Dividends and Capital Gain Worksheet
The lesser of either /taxableIncome or /qualifiedDividendsAndLongTermCapitalGain. Line 10 - Qualified Dividends and Capital Gain Worksheet
Subtract /amountOfNetGainsTaxedAt0 from /qdcgWorksheetLine10. Line 12 - Qualified Dividends and Capital Gain Worksheet
Lesser of Lines 1 and 13 Line 14 - Qualified Dividends and Capital Gain Worksheet
Subtract Line 15 from Line 14 to calculate remaining amount eligible to be taxed at 15%. Line 16 - Qualified Dividends and Capital Gain Worksheet
Lesser amount when comparing /qdcgWorksheetLine12 and /remainingTaxableFor15, Line 12 vs Line 16 Line 17 - Qualified Dividends and Capital Gain Worksheet
Tax withholding based on 15% tax of eligible qualified dividends. Line 18 - Qualified Dividends and Capital Gain Worksheet
Subtract Line 19 from Line 10 to calculate remaining amount eligible to be taxed at 20%. Line 20 - Qualified Dividends and Capital Gain Worksheet
Tax withholding based on 20% tax of eligible qualified dividends. Line 21 -Qualified Dividends and Capital Gain Worksheet

https://www.irs.gov/credits-deductions/individuals/adoption-credit#qualifiedexpenses Qualified adoption expenses for the credit and exclusion are reasonable and necessary expenses you paid to adopt include: - Adoption fees - Attorney fees - Court costs - Travel expenses including meals and lodging - Other expenses directly for the legal adoption - Expenses you paid before an eligible child has been identified, such as home study fees
Expenses aren’t eligible if they are: - To adopt your spouse’s child - For a surrogate parent arrangement - Allowed for another federal tax credit or deduction - Paid by a federal, state or local program - Reimbursed by your employer

Fact.Name: Number of Adoption credit eligible children

Fact.Description: Phaseout threshold for MAGI
Form 8839 Line 7. Maximum total adoption credit = max per child * number of children
Maximum total adoption credit = max per child * number of children
Form 8839 Line 6, aggregated
Form 8839 Line 8
Form 8839 Line 9
Form 8839 Line 10
Form 8839 Line 12 and 11a (aggregated)
Form 8839 Line 13 and Line 11c (aggregated)
Form 8839 Line 14
Form 8839 Line 15
Form 8839 Line 16
Form 8839 Line 18
Maximum annual deductible and other out-of-pocket expenses for family. We are agnostic to filing status so double to 17000 and do not take MAGI into account for simplified computation
HSA total deductible amount
Deduction for contributions to traditional IRAs (excluding payroll deductions)

Max contribution for traditional IRAs (excluding payroll deductions) is $8000 per year. $7000 is the limit for those under 50, but we only know if someone is under 65 or not We are agnostic to filing status so double to 16000 and do not take MAGI into account for simplified computation
Deduction for contributions to traditional IRAs (excluding payroll deductions)
Moving expenses for members of armed services
Alimony paid (pre 2019 divorce)
Penalty for early withdrawal of savings
Self-employed health insurance contributions for both self and spouse. Implements Form 7206
Business credits for reservists, performing artists, and fee-based government officials
Self-employed contributions to eligible retirement plans.

The adjustments to income used in the calculation of non-IRA deduction MAGIs to determine deducible amounts. Used in the Student Loan Interest Deduction and Social Security Benefits Worksheets. This should include be a sum of Schedule 1, lines 11 through 20, and 23 and 25
Adjustments to income to report on line 10 of Form 1040 and line 26 of Schedule 1.

Fact.Name: Qualified Tip Income
Fact.Description: OB3 §70201; IRC §224. Qualified tip income

Fact.Name: Maximum qualified tip deduction
Fact.Description: The maximum amount one is allowed to deduct of their tip income

Fact.Name: Do tips exceed self employment income?
Fact.Description: OB3 Section 70201 temporarily creating §224(c): Deduction may not exceed individual’s net income (without regard to this deduction) from the trade or business in which the tips were earned.

Fact.Name: Taxpayer eligible for qualified tip deduction
Fact.Description: Is the taxpayer eligible for the qualified tip deduction?

Fact.Name: Qualified Tip Deduction Phase Out
Fact.Description: The amount allowable as a deduction under shall be reduced (but not below zero) by $100 for each $1,000 by which the taxpayer’s MAGI exceeds $150,000 ($300,000 in the case of a joint return).

Fact.Name: Qualified tip deduction
Fact.Description: OB3 Section 70201: Qualified tip deductions Example: Single filer with 160k MAGI and 20k qualified tips. 150k applicable tip deduction threshold (20,000 excess/1000)*100 = 2,000 phase out 20,000 tip - 2,000 phase out = 18,000 applicable deduction

Fact.Name: SALT tax payment applicable threshold
Fact.Description: Maximum amount that can be itemized for SALT deduction
Total of all SALT payment items, up to MFS and non-MFS thresholds
Phased down the SALT cap for taxpayers making above $500,000, at a 30% rate. Floor the phase down to $10k (non-MFS) or $5k (MFS), which is the max high earners can deduct

Maximum possible deduction of SALT payment items, with applicable limits and phase downs Applicable limitation amount = max{40K – (0.3 * max{MAGI – (filing status specific threshold amount), 0 }, 10K}
Total deduction of SALT payment items, with applicable limits and phase downs SALT deduction = min{SALT taxes, filing status specific applicable limitation amount}
NIIT Filing Status Income Threshold
Form 8960 Line 17

Qualified education expenses are amounts paid in TaxYear for tuition and fees required for the student’s enrollment or attendance at an eligible educational institution. It doesn’t matter whether the expenses were paid in cash, by check, by credit or debit card, or with borrowed funds.For course-related books, supplies, and equipment, only certain expenses qualify. -> American opportunity credit: Qualified education expenses include amounts paid for tuition, fees, and course materials, which include books, supplies, and equipment needed for a course of study, whether or not the materials are purchased from the educational institution as a condition of enrollment or attendance. -> Lifetime learning credit: Qualified education expenses include amounts paid for books, supplies, and equipment only if required to be paid to the institution as a condition of enrollment or attendance. https://www.irs.gov/instructions/i8863#en_US_2025_publink53002gd0e494
Tuition and required enrollment fees (including amounts required to be paid to the institution for course-related books, supplies, and equipment)
Form 8863 Line 27
Form 8863 Line 28
Form 8863 Line 29
Form 8863 Line 30
Form 8863 Line 1, for all qualifying students
Form 8863 Line 1, for all qualifying students
Form 8863 Line 2 and line 13, thresholds by filing status
Form 8863 Line 4 and Line 15
Form 8863 Line 5 and 16, by filing status
Form 8863 Line 6 and Line 17
Form 8863 Line 7
Form 8863 Line 8. Input on Form 1040 Line 29
Form 8863 Line 31
Form 8863 Line 9. Input on Line 2 of Credit Limit Worksheet
Form 8863 Line 11. Lesser of total LLC expenses for all students and $10,000
Form 8863 Line 12
Form 8863 Line 18
The tax year of the return.
The number of days in the tax year: 365 or 366.
The number of days in the prior tax year: 365 or 366.
Whether or not the current tax year is a leap year.
Form 1040 Line 26. The taxpayer's estimated taxes paid for the entire tax year.
Form 461 line 15
If line 12 is a negative number, enter it here as a positive number. If line 12 is a positive number, enter it here as a negative number
Form 461 Line 16. Form 1040 Schedule 1 line 8p
The count of the number of blindness or old age items among the filers.

Fact.Name: Secondary Filer Additional Standard Deduction Items
Fact.Description: Additional Standard Deduction Items from the secondary filer

Fact.Name: Additional standard deduction multiplier
Fact.Description: The amount to add to the standard deduction for each additional item. Rev Proc .14 Standard Deduction §63(f)

Fact.Name: Additional standard deduction
Fact.Description: The additional amount to add to the standard deduction to account for blindness or old age.
A temporary "feature flag". Manually change this if you want to use the original TY2025 numbers IRS announced. Useful when comparing to historical outcomes like the QA spreadsheet.

Fact.Name: Married Filing Jointly Standard Deduction
Fact.Description: Standard Deduction for filers who are married filing jointly. Rev Proc .15 Standard Deduction §1(j)(2)(A)

Fact.Name: Single or Married Filing Separately Standard Deduction
Fact.Description: Standard Deduction for filers who are married filing separately or single. Rev Proc .15 Standard Deduction §1(j)(2)(C) §1(j)(2)(D)

Fact.Name: HoH Standard Deduction
Fact.Description: Standard Deduction for filers who are filing HoH. Rev Proc .15 Standard Deduction §1(j)(2)(B)
The amount of the standard deduction based on filing status alone.
The amount to use as the standard deduction if a filer cannot be claimed as a dependent.
The alternative amount to use as the standard deduction if a filer can be claimed as a dependent. This amount is not used if it exceeds the normal amount of the standard deduction.
The alternative amount to use as the standard deduction if a filer can be claimed as a dependent. This amount is not used if it exceeds the normal amount of the standard deduction.
The amount of the standard deduction
Foreign tax credit. Form 1116
Credit for child and dependent care expenses from Form 2441, line 11.
Education credits from Form 8863, line 19
Retirement savings contributions credit. Form 8880
Residential clean energy credit from Form 5695, line 15
Energy efficient home improvement credit from Form 5695, line 32
General business credit. Form 3800
Credit for prior year minimum tax. Form 8801
Non-refundable portion of Adoption credit. Form 8839
Credit for the elderly or disabled. Schedule R
Reserved for future use
Clean vehicle credit. Form 8936
Mortgage interest credit. Form 8396
District of Columbia first-time homebuyer credit. Form 8859
Qualified electric vehicle credit. Form 8834
Alternative fuel vehicle refueling property credit. Form 8911
Credit to holders of tax credit bonds. Form 8912
Form 8978, line 14.
Credit for previously owned clean vehicles. Form 8936
AMT Credit
Other nonrefundable credits.
Total other nonrefundable credits.
Net premium tax credit. Form 8962
Amount paid with request for extension to file
Excess social security and tier 1 RRTA tax withheld
Credit for federal tax on fuels. Form 4136
Other payments or refundable credits
Schedule 3 Line 8. Total of all non-refundable credits
Schedule 3 Line 5. Total of all other payments and refundable credits
Current tax year
Previous tax year
Tax year minus two years

Fact.Name: Filing Status Options
Fact.Description: Options for filing status

Fact.Name: Filing status
Fact.Description: The filing status selected by the taxpayer.
Is taxpayer filing status single
Is taxpayer filing status MFS
Is taxpayer filing status MFJ
Is taxpayer filing status HOH
Is taxpayer filing status QSS

Fact.Name: Interest paid on passenger vehicle loan
Fact.Description: How much interest have you paid on the passenger vehicle loan during the tax year?

Fact.Name: Eligible for Qualified Passenger Vehicle Loan Interest Deduction
Fact.Description: Taxpayer eligibility for

Fact.Name: Maximum qualified personal vehicle loan deduction
Fact.Description: The maximum amount one is allowed to deduct because of qualified personal vehicle loan interest payments

Fact.Name: Qualified Personal Vehicle Loan Amount Phase Out
Fact.Description:
The amount which is otherwise allowable as a deduction under subsection (a) as qualified passenger vehicle loan interest (determined without regard to this clause and after the application of clause (i)) shall be reduced (but not below zero) by $200 for each $1,000 (or portion thereof) by which the modified adjusted gross income of the taxpayer for the taxable year exceeds $100,000 ($200,000 in the case of a joint return).
Phaseout amount for the qualified personal vehicle loan deduction: phaseOutAmount= 200*(filing= 200*(filing status specific MAGI/1000)status specific MAGI/1000)

Fact.Name: Tentative amount of the personal vehicle loan interest deduction
Fact.Description: The lesser of 10000 or actual interest paid minus phase out amount, with a lowest possible value of 0

Fact.Name: Actual amount of the personal vehicle loan interest deduction
Fact.Description: Defaults to 0 if QPVLID section is not complete
Net profit loss, reported as Line 31 on Schedule C and line 2 of Schedule SE for self.
Net profit loss, reported as Line 31 on Schedule C and line 2 of Schedule SE for spouse.
Specific calculation on line 4a of Schedule SE (Form 1040) to multiply net self employment income by 92.35%.
Combine lines 4a and 4b. If less than $400, 0 and no self-employment tax.
5a) Enter your church employee income from Form W-2. See instructions for definition of church employee income.
5b) Multiply line 5a by 92.35% (0.9235). If less than $100, enter -0-
Line 4c + Line 5b

Sum of 8a + 8b + 8c 8a) Total social security wages and tips (total of boxes 3 and 7 on Form(s) W-2) and railroad retirement (tier 1) compensation. 8b) Unreported tips subject to social security tax from Form 4137, line 10 8c) Wages subject to social security tax from Form 8919, line 10
Greater of zero or Line 7 - Line 8d
Self-employment tax multiplied by the Social Security tax rate from line 10 on Schedule SE (Form 1040). 12.4 * MIN(line6,line9)
Line 10 *2.9% (medicare self employment tax portion)
Self employment tax (line 10 + line 11) Enter here and on Schedule 2 (Form 1040), line 4, or Form 1040-SS, Part I, line 3
Self Employment Tax Deduction. Multiply line 12 by 50% (0.50). Schedule SE line 13, Schedule 1 (Form 1040), line 15
Specific calculation on line 4a of Schedule SE (Form 1040) to multiply net self employment income by 92.35%.
Combine lines 4a and 4b. If less than $400, 0 and no self-employment tax.
5a) Enter your church employee income from Form W-2. See instructions for definition of church employee income.
5b) Multiply line 5a by 92.35% (0.9235). If less than $100, enter -0-
Add line 4c and 5b for spouse

Sum of 8a + 8b + 8c 8a) Total social security wages and tips (total of boxes 3 and 7 on Form(s) W-2) and railroad retirement (tier 1) compensation. 8b) Unreported tips subject to social security tax from Form 4137, line 10 8c) Wages subject to social security tax from Form 8919, line 10
Greater of zero or Line 7 - Line 8d
Self-employment tax multiplied by the Social Security tax rate from line 10 on Schedule SE (Form 1040). 12.4 * MIN(line6,line9)
Line 10 *2.9% (medicare self employment tax portion)
Self employment tax (line 10 + line 11) Enter here and on Schedule 2 (Form 1040), line 4, or Form 1040-SS, Part I, line 3
Self Employment Tax Deduction. Multiply line 12 by 50% (0.50). Schedule SE line 13, Schedule 1 (Form 1040), line 15
Self employment tax combined for both self and spouse. Entered on Schedule 2 line 4 and Form 1040-SS Part I, line 3
Self Employment Tax Deduction combined for both self and spouse. Form 1040 Schedule 1 Line 15

Total amount paid in 2024 for health insurance coverage established under your business(or the S corporation in which you were a more-than-2% shareholder) for 2024 for you, your spouse,and your dependents. Excludes: 1) Amounts for any month you were eligible to participate in a health plan subsidized by your employer or your spouse’s employer or the employer of either your dependent or your child who was under the age of 27 at the end of 2024. 2) Any amounts paid, not to exceed $3,000, from retirement plan distributions that were non taxablebecause you are a retired public safety officer. 3) Any payments for qualified long-term care insurance (see line 2)
Qualified long-term care insurance contract payments or premiums. Included in Line 1 per content
Total SE health insurance and long term care premiums
Net profit and any other earned income from the trade or business under which the insurance plan is established. Don’t include Conservation Reserve Program payments exempt from self-employment tax.
Schedule C line 31, Schedule SE line 2. Total net profit of self-employment income combined for both self and spouse
Percentage of profit allocated to the business under which the insurance plan is established
Line 6 multiplied by deductible portion of SE tax
Net profit - applicable portion of SE tax deduction
SE SEP SIMPLE or qualified plans under the insurance plan is established
Medicare wages from an S corp in which you are >2% shareholder in which the insurance plan is established
Form 2555, line 45
Medicare wages from an S corp in which you are >2% shareholder in which the insurance plan is established
Final self employment health insurance deduction amount
Form 7206 line 14. Self-employed health insurance deduction.

Self-employed retirement plan deduction. SEP SIMPLE or other qualified plan (Schedule C net profit - deductible portion of SE tax) x (plan contribution rate/ 100% + plan contribution rate) Because we don't know what the plan contribution rate is, for all intents and purposes, the max SE retirement plan deduction is net self-employment profit - SE tax deduction https://www.irs.gov/retirement-plans/self-employed-individuals-calculating-your-own-retirement-plan-contribution-and-deduction

Fact.Name: Minimum Deduction for Active QBI
Fact.Description: OB3 70111, amending IRC 26 Section 199A(i): In the case of an applicable taxpayer for any taxable year, the deduction allowed under subsection(a) for the taxable year shall be equal to the greater of—(A) the amount of such deduction determined without regard to this subsection, or (B) $400.

Fact.Name: Minimum QBI for Base Deduction
Fact.Description: OB3 70111, amending IRC 26 Section 199A(i): (2) APPLICABLE TAXPAYER.—For purposes of this sub-section—(A) IN GENERAL.—The term ‘applicable taxpayer’means, with respect to any taxable year, a taxpayer whose aggregate qualified business income with respect to all active qualified trades or businesses of the taxpayer for such taxable year is at least $1,000.
Form 8995-A Line 3
Form 8995-A Line 15 (aggregated) and Line 16
Form 8995-A Line 19 = Line 17- Line 18 = Line 3 - Line 10
QBI Phase In Percentage, rounded to 2 decimals
Form 8895-A Line 26, flows into Line 12
Form 8895-A Line 39.
Tentative QBI is over applicable taxpayer threshold of $1,000

Fact.Name: Derived Qualified Business Income Deduction
Fact.Description: Section 199A: qualified business income deduction. The deduction allows eligible taxpayers to deduct up to 20 percent of their QBI, plus 20 percent of qualified real estate investment trust (REIT) dividends and qualified publicly traded partnership (PTP) income. Income earned through a C corporation or by providing services as an employee is not eligible for the deduction. For more information on what qualifies as a trade or business, see Determining your qualified trades or businesses in the Instructions for Form 8995-A or Form 8995.

Fact.Name: Qualified Business Income Deduction
Fact.Description: Taxpayer provided QBID if they want to override the derived amount, else the derived amount

Fact.Name: Adjusted Gross Income
Fact.Description: Total income to report on line 11 of Form 1040.
Taxable income as reported on line 15 of Form 1040. Should not be negative.
Taxable income without the QBI Deduction, with coordination with OB3 70111 to exclude limitation on itemized deduction

Fact.Name: Modified Adjusted Gross Income for OB3 Below Line Deductions
Fact.Description: Adjusted gross income of the taxpayer for the taxable year increased by any amount excluded from gross income under section 911, 931, or 933.

Fact.Name: Rounded taxable income
Fact.Description: Income less than $100,000 uses the tax tables from the Instructions for Form 1040. This fact selects the "midpoint" that corresponds to each entry in the tax tables, for use in calculating tentative tax.
Tentative tax as reported on line 16 of Form 1040, excluding any qualified dividends or capital gains. Rev Proc .01 Tax Rate Tables §1(j)(2)(A|B|C|D)
Remaining taxable income after subtracting qualified dividends and long-term capital gains. Line 5 - Qualified Dividends and Capital Gain Worksheet

Fact.Name: Rounded taxable income minus net gains

Fact.Description: Line 22 Qualified Dividends Capital Gains Worksheet. Required to calculate Form 1040 line 16 when qualified dividends and capital gains are present
Tentative tax for all taxable income + QDCGs Line 23 - Qualified Dividends and Capital Gain Worksheet

Lesser of tentative tax without net capital gains and tentative tax with capital gains. /tentativeTaxFromTaxableIncomeWithNetCapitalGains will be smaller than /tentativeTaxFromTaxableIncomeWithoutNetGains if net capital gains is negative (net capital loss), in which case we want to tax the taxpayer at a lower rate based on the loss. Line 16 Form 1040 Line 25 - Qualified Dividends and Capital Gain Worksheet
Schedule 2 Line 3. Form 1040 line 17. Summation of Lines 1a-z and 2, all of which are not supported in TWE today
Schedule 2 Line 21. Form 1040 line 23.

Fact.Name: Total tentative tax
Fact.Description: Form 1040 Line 18. Total tentative tax

Fact.Name: Total tentative tax after applying non-refundable credits, but before applying refundable credits.
Fact.Description: Form 1040 Line 22.
Form 1040 Line 24. Taxes from taxable income minus non refundable credits plus other taxes, including self-employment tax.
Form 1040 Line 33

Form 1040 Line 37. Estimated total taxes owed. The tax liability minus the total credits and total advance payments made during the tax year. Negative value = overpayment = refund Positive value = underpayment = balance due
Threshold at which the top tax bracket (37%) applies by filing status. https://www.irs.gov/pub/irs-drop/rp-25-32.pdf

Fact.Name: Modified Adjusted Gross Income for Student Loan Interest Deduction
Fact.Description: This is AGI, but without student loan interest deduction.

Taxable income for calculating itemized deduction cap for itemizers. By default excludes standard deduction because the taxpayer is itemizing, as well as itemized deduction to avoid circular dependency.
Excess taxable income over the 37% tax rate. Different brackets apply depending on filing status

Schedule SE Line 7. Maximum amount of combined wages and self-employment earnings subject to social security tax or the 6.2% portion of the 7.65% railroad retirement (tier 1) tax for 2026. See https://www.ssa.gov/oact/cola/cbb.html
Schedule 8812 Line 21
Schedule 8812 Line 23
Elderly and Disabled tax credit, no logic in place for actually calculating the applicable amount.
The taxpayers's jobs
Whether this job is attributed to the primary filer or spouse if MFJ
Whether this job was held for the entire tax year.

Fact.Name: Overtime Compensation Excluding Qualified Tip Overtime Comp
Fact.Description: OB3 §70202 IRC §225(c)(2). Overtime compensation excluding all qualified tips defined in IRC §224 that were received as part of qualified overtime compensation
Total amount of overtime paid to the taxpayer, with no regard to the excess over normal wages
Multiplier provided by the taxpayer that indicates whether they are paid at a time and a half (1.5) or double time (2.0) overtime rate

Fact.Name: Qualified Tip Income
Fact.Description: OB3 §70201; IRC §224. Qualified tip income
The first date the job was held in the current calendar year, if not an all year job.
The last date the job was held in the current calendar year, if not an all year job.
The date which the most recent pay period ended on for this particular job
The date on which pay was received, corresponding to the most recent pay period that ended
The year-to-date withholding from a specific job
The average withholding from a specific job per pay period

Fact.Name: Pay Frequency
Fact.Description: The frequency that the job pays the user. And the length of pay periods.
The total amount of bonus per year for a specific job
The total income from a specific job
Pre-tax deductions from a specific job
Pre-tax deductions from a specific job
Pre-tax deductions from a specific job
Pre-tax deductions from a specific job
The pre-tax deductions being taken out of a particular paycheck. These are used to get an accurate calculation on the Pub15T withholding tables, not to calculate overall tax burden.
Flow control: whether to ask start and end dates for this job.
The first date the job was held in the current calendar year.
The last date the job was held in the current calendar year.
Does the filer hold this job at the end of the year?

Fact.Name: Pay Frequency Options
Fact.Description: Options for pay frequency
Length of pay periods matching the Floor of Publication 15-T values.
Threshold for changing bonus withholdings from 22% to 37%
Whether the filer is 65 or older at the end of the tax year.
Whether the filer is 65 or older at the end of the tax year.
Whether the primary filer will greater than or equal to 25 at the end of the tax year.
Whether the secondary filer will greater than or equal to 25 at the end of the tax year.
Primary taxpayer self-certifies their eligibility for the senior deduction
Secondary taxpayer self-certifies their eligibility for the senior deduction
Either the primary or secondary taxpayer could be eligible for the senior deduction
Whether the primary taxpayer could be eligible for the senior deduction
Whether the secondary taxpayer could be eligible for the senior deduction

Fact.Name: Senior Deduction Secondary Filer Eligibility
Fact.Description: OB3 Section 70103 temporarily amending 151(d)(5)(3)(c)(ii)(II) taxpayer's spouse, if such spouse has attained age 65 before the close of the taxable year.

Fact.Name: Senior Deduction Amount Phase Out
Fact.Description:
Phaseout amount for the senior deduction. phaseOutAmount = excess * 0.06.
In the case of any taxpayer for any taxable year, the $6,000 amount in clause (i) shall be reduced (but not below zero) by 6 percent of so much of the taxpayer’s modified adjusted gross income as exceeds $75,000 ($150,000 in the case of a joint return).
Example 1):Single filer with MAGI of $85,500. This amount exceeds the relevant threshold ($75,000) by $10,500. Therefore, the $6,000 amount is reduced by $630 (6% of $10,500) to $5,370.Example 2):Joint filer with MAGI of $160,500. Both filers qualify for the deduction based on their age and SSN. Their MAGI exceeds the relevant threshold ($150,000) by $10,500. Therefore, the$6,000 amount is reduced by $630 (6% of $10,500) to $5,370. Because both the primary and secondary filer are eligible for the deduction, the deduction amount is $10,740 ($5,370 per qualified individual multiplied by 2 qualified individuals).

Fact.Name: Maximum senior deduction amount
Fact.Description: The maximum amount a single taxpayer can deduct. Doubled for MFJ if both taxpayers are eligible

Fact.Name: Maximum senior deduction amount
Fact.Description: The maximum amount a single taxpayer can deduct. Doubled for MFJ if both taxpayers are eligible

Fact.Name: Actual Amount of Senior Deduction
Fact.Description: Actual amount of senior deduction that can be applied against MAGI. amount = (max amount based on eligibility)- (excess * 0.06)
Total other income minus adjustments to income; the net change to be entered on Forms W4 Lines 4a (when positive) or 4b (when negative; use absolute value).
Form W-4 Line 4(a); also Pub 15-T Worksheet 1A Line 1d.
Form W-4 Line 4(b); also Pub 15-T Worksheet 1A Line 1f.
W-4 Line 3. Also used on Line 3c of Pub. 15-T Worksheet 1A
Overwritable today fact

Fact.Name: Student loan interest amount
Fact.Description: The amount of interest that the TP affirms that they or their spouse paid on qualify student loans

Fact.Name: Maximum student loan deduction
Fact.Description: The maximum amount one is allowed to deduct because of student loan interest payments

Fact.Name: Max income for a non-MFJ filer student loan interest deduction
Fact.Description: The maximum MAGI that a non-MFJ filer can have and still receive the Student Loan Interest Deduction

Fact.Name: Max income for an MFJ filer student loan interest deduction
Fact.Description: The maximum MAGI that an MFJ filer can have and still receive the Student Loan Interest Deduction

Fact.Name: Student Loan Phase Out Start
Fact.Description: The MAGI at which the student loan interest deduction begins to phase out for non-MFJ filers Rev Proc 30 Interest on Education Loans §221(b)(2)(B)

Fact.Name: Student Loan Phase Out Start for MFJ filers
Fact.Description: The MAGI at which the student loan interest deduction begins to phase out for MFJ filers. Rev Proc 30 Interest on Education Loans §221(b)(2)(B)

Fact.Name: Cannot deduct student loan interest because of income or filing status
Fact.Description: The TP is ineligible to deduct student loan interest because of income or filing status

Fact.Name: Income too high for student loan interest deduction
Fact.Description: Whether income is too high for a non-mfj filer to claim the deduction

Fact.Name: Income too high for student loan interest deduction
Fact.Description: Whether income is too high for an MFJ filer to claim the deduction

Fact.Name: Student Loan Phase Out Range
Fact.Description: The denominator of the phaseout calculation for the student loan deduction for non-MFJ filers

Fact.Name: Student Loan Phase Out Range for MFJ filers
Fact.Description: The denominator of the phaseout calculation for the student loan deduction for MFJ filers

Fact.Name: Student Loan Amount Phase Out Multiplier
Fact.Description: Phaseout amount for the student loan deduction. Described in instructions for 1040, schedule 1, line 21. Corresponds with Line 8 of the Student Loan Interest Deduction Worksheet. Returns 0 if there is no phaseout

Fact.Name: Student Loan Interest Deduction Amount
Fact.Description: The amount of the student loan interest deduction. Line 21 of Form 1040 Schedule 1, Line 33 Calculated per requirements on Student Loan Interest Deduction Worksheet
Total sum of non-refundable and refundable credits.
Form 1040 Line 21. Schedule 3 Line 8 + CTC/ODC nonrefundable portion.
Form 1040 Line 32. Schedule 3 Line 15 + EITC,ACTC, AOTC, refundable portion of Adoption

Fact.Name: Itemized deductions
Fact.Description: Total amount of itemized deductions minus 2/37 of the excess taxable income of the top tax bracket. For taxpayers not in that bracket, the excess will be $0. estimated itemized deductions

Fact.Name: Total amount of itemized deductions
Fact.Description: Total amount of itemized deductions that the taxpayer estimates for this tax year. There are edge cases for taxpayers in the highest tax bracket that must apply before we calculate the final amount of itemized deductions

OB3 70111 adding: IRC Section 68: (a) In general In the case of an individual, the amount of the itemized deductions otherwise allowable for the taxable year (determined without regard to this section) shall be reduced by 2⁄37 of the lesser of (1) such amount of itemized deductions, or (2) so much of the taxable income of the taxpayer for the taxable year (determined without regard to this section and increased by such amount of itemized deductions) as exceeds the dollar amount at which the 37 percent rate bracket under section 1 begins with respect to the taxpayer. n.b. this definition is ignored for purposes of QBI deduction calculation per OB3

Fact.Name: Qualified mortgage interest and investment interest expenses
Fact.Description: Qualified mortgage interest including points (if you're the buyer) and Investment interest (limited to your net investment income)

Fact.Name: State and Local Taxes
Fact.Description: There are four types of deductible nonbusiness SALT taxes: 1) State, local, and foreign income taxes; 2) State and local general sales taxes; 3) State and local real estate taxes; 4) State and local personal property taxes

Fact.Name: Charitable Contributions
Fact.Description: Charitable contributions to qualified organizations

Fact.Name: Total of all medical and dental expenses
Fact.Description: Total expenses that exceed 7.5% of your adjusted gross income. Can include the total estimated amount paid for health insurance premiums, not including any amount the employer pays

Fact.Name: Total of all medical and dental expenses
Fact.Description: Total expenses that exceed 7.5% of your adjusted gross income. Can include the total estimated amount paid for health insurance premiums, not including any amount the employer pays

Fact.Name: Total of all charitable contributions
Fact.Description:
OB3 70425(a) amends IRC 170(b)(1) to only allow this itemized deduction for individuals whose aggregate charitable contributions are in excess of %0.5 of their AGI
MIN of A) Charitable contributions - (0.005 * MAX(contribution base, 0); or B) 0.6 * contribution base

Fact.Name: Total of all charitable contributions
Fact.Description: Total of all /charitableContributions items

Fact.Name: Total of all casualty loss items
Fact.Description: Casualty and theft losses relating to your home, household items, and vehicles if the loss took place in a federally declared disaster area

Fact.Name: Total of all other itemized deductions

Fact.Description: Savers credit, no logic in place for actually calculating the applicable amount.
Whether the taxpayer has declared they are done adding new income
Form 1040 Line 3b. Ordinary dividends income
Unemployment insurance income
Form 1040 Line 2a. Non-taxable interest
Form 1040 Line 2b. Interest income excluding non-taxable interest
Short term capital gains income
Long term capital gains income
Form 1040 Line 3a. Amount of qualified dividends estimated by tax payer.
Form 1040 Line 7
Qualified Dividends + lesser of long term and gross capital gains. Used in QBI Deduction and QDCG Worksheet
Taxable income from scholarships
Alimony received income
S-corp non-passive income (Schedule K1)
Net Schedule E income
Non-specific other income
Schedule E Line 21
Schedule E Line 23b
If TP has MFS filing status and lived with their spouse for the entirety of the TY

Fact.Name: OB3 70204: Distribution from Trump Account as defined by IRC 530A
Fact.Description: The term "Trump account" means an individual retirement account (as defined in section 408(a)) which is not designated as a Roth IRA and which meets certain investment requirements

Fact.Name: § 911 Foreign Earned Income Exclusion- Foreign Earned Income
§ 931 - Income from sources within Guam, American Samoa, or the Northern Mariana Islands
§ 933. Income from sources within Puerto Rico

Fact.Description: Total income for all pensions
Total income for all pensions for spouse
Total income from all pensions for both self and spouse
Total income for all jobs
Total income for all jobs for spouse
Total income for all jobs for both self and spouse

Earned Income includes: Wages, salary, or tips where federal income taxes are withheld on Form W-2, box 1 Income from a job where your employer didn’t withhold tax (such as gig economy work) including: Driving a car for booked rides or deliveries Running errands or doing tasks Selling goods online Providing creative or professional services Providing other temporary, on-demand or freelance work Money made from self-employment, including if you: Own or operate a business or farm Are a minister or member of a religious order Are a statutory employee and have income Benefits from a union strike Certain disability benefits you got before you were the minimum retirement age Nontaxable Combat Pay (Form W-2, box 12 with code Q)
Earned income does not include: Pay you got for work when you were an inmate in a penal institution Interest and dividends Pensions or annuities Social Security Unemployment benefits Alimony Child support
Other sources of income. Taxable portion of SSA benefits not included. Used to calculate taxable portion onf SSA benefits
Used for W-4 calculations
Sum of 1z, 2b, 3b, 4b, 5b, 7, and 8 on the 1040 (Line 9 - 6b)
Form 1040 Line 9

Fact.Name: Number of CDCC qualifying persons
Fact.Description: The number of people who are eligible for the CDCC. Form 2441 Line 2a, aggregated
Child and Dependent Care qualifying expenses. Form 2441 Line 2d, aggregated

Fact.Name: Maximum Claimable Expense With One Qualifying Person
Maximum Claimable Expense With Two or More Qualifying Persons
Maximum Allowed Credit With One Qualifying Person
Maximum Allowed Credit With Two or More Qualifying Persons
Minimum Allowed Percentage for Tax Year 2024
Maximum Allowed Percentage for Tax Year 2024
Care Expenses Fall Off Threshold
Fact.Description: The threshold at which the care expenses decimal amount begins reducing

Fact.Name: Care Expenses Fall Off Threshold
Fact.Description: The threshold at which the care expenses decimal amount stops reducing

Fact.Name: The maximum claimable qualifying expenses
Fact.Description:
The maximum claimable qualifying expenses. Also called the Allowed Cared For Amount.
Form 2441, Line 27

Fact.Name: Qualifying Expenses
Fact.Description: The capped sum of all qualifying expenses. Form 2441, Line 3
Lowest of Earned income of MFJ filers. Form 2441 Line 5
Lower of earned income (regardless of filing status). Lesser of Form 2441 Line 4 and Line 5
The lower of the earned income values and capped qualifying expenses Form 2441, Line 6

Fact.Name: Care Expenses Decimal Amount
Fact.Description: Care Expenses Decimal Amount. Form 2441, Line 8. Line 7 is AGI

Fact.Name: Tentative Expense Amount
Fact.Description: Form 2441, Line 9a

Fact.Name: Credit For Prior Year Expenses
Fact.Description: Credit for any expenses incurred in the prior tax year. Not currently supported, so this is set as zero. Form 2441, Line 9b
Form 2441, Line 9c

Fact.Name: Tax Liability Credit Limit
Fact.Description: The maximum possible credit the filer can receive for CDCC, calculated using the Credit Limit Worksheet

Credit for child and dependent care expenses. Smaller of line 9c or line 10
Form 2441 Line 11. Input on Schedule 3 Line 2
The end of year amount withheld for this job if nothing is changed.
This amount this job will pay for the rest of the year.
Bonus withholdings are 22% if less than 1,000,000 and 37% on the sum exceeding 1,000,000. Ref: Pub 15, Section 7.
Bonus withholdings are 22% if less than 1,000,000 and 37% on the sum exceeding 1,000,000. Ref: Pub 15, Section 7.
Bonus withholdings are 22% if less than 1,000,000 and 37% on the sum exceeding 1,000,000. Ref: Pub 15, Section 7.
Line 2h of Pub. 15-T Worksheet 1A. The amount from the Annual Method Percentage table.
The total amount that will be withheld from the paycheck if the W-4 is submitted today with no additional withholdings. This is also referred to as the "do-nothing withholding."
The amount that will be withheld in the time between today and when the W-4 is expected to be processed.
The earnings if the job duration were all year.

The job selected to have Line 4a populated on a new W4. It is the year end job with the highest proportion of income among all year jobs, or if there are no year end jobs it is the job with highest proportion of standard withholding for the rest of the year.
Date when we expect the W4 chagnes to take effect. 15 days after the current date
Jobs that are ended or end too soon are not candidates for extra withholding on a new W4.
The job selected for extra withholding on a new W4. The W4 refers to this as the "highest paying job".
Total actual withholding for all jobs
The amount withheld for all jobs if nothing is changed.
The rest-of-year withholding gap: taxes owed if nothing is changed.
The tax gap once W-4s are updated and we account for the remaining pay periods. Effectively 1040 Line 37 - Lines 25d
Line 1g of Pub 15-T Worksheet 1A
Line 1a of Pub 15-T Worksheet 1A
Line 1i of Pub 15-T Worksheet 1A

Standard withholding rate schedules for use when Form W-4 Step 2 box is not checked. Line 2g of Pub. 15-T Worksheet 1A. These amounts are generally a base withholding amount plus a percentage of the excess of Annual Adjusted Wages Amount. The base withholding captures the total withholding for all the brackets below the one that the AAWA falls into.
Non-refundable credit for contributions of individuals to scholarship granting organizations, no logic in place for actually calculating the applicable amount
Max amount of Credit for contributions of individuals to scholarship granting organizations

26 U.S. Code § 25F: (b) Limitations (1) In general The credit allowed under subsection (a) to any taxpayer for any taxable year shall not exceed $1,700. (2) Reduction based on State credit The amount allowed as a credit under subsection (a) for a taxable year shall be reduced by the amount allowed as a credit on any State tax return of the taxpayer for qualified contributions made by the taxpayer during the taxable year.
The maximum amount of EITC that can be received based on the taxpayer's AGI.

Fact.Name: Rounded earned income for EITC without combat pay
Fact.Description: In order to match the EITC table from Publication 596, we must round the income to match the rows, which use increments of $50, calculating the amount of the credit for the midpoint of row (i.e. $25, $75, $125, etc.).

Fact.Name: Rounded AGI for EITC
Fact.Description: In order to match the EITC table from Publication 596, we must round the income to match the rows, which use increments of $50, calculating the amount of the credit for the midpoint of row (i.e. $25, $75, $125, etc.).

Fact.Name: EITC maximum credit amount
Fact.Description: The maximum amount of EITC a taxpayer can recieve, depending on the number of qualifying children.

Fact.Name: Maximum phase in amount with 0 children
Fact.Description: The earned income amount which provides the max EITC with 0 qualifying children Rev Proc .06 Earned Income Credit §32(b)

Fact.Name: Maximum phase in amount with 1 child
Fact.Description: The earned income amount which provides the max EITC with 1 qualifying child Rev Proc .06 Earned Income Credit §32(b)

Fact.Name: Maximum phase in amount with 2 or more children
Fact.Description: The earned income amount which provides the max EITC with 2 or more qualifying children Rev Proc .06 Earned Income Credit §32(b)

Fact.Name: Maximum EITC amount with 0 children
Fact.Description: The maximum earned income tax credit with 0 qualifying children

Fact.Name: Maximum EITC amount with 1 child
Fact.Description: The maximum earned income tax credit with 1 qualifying child

Fact.Name: Maximum EITC amount with 2 children
Fact.Description: The maximum earned income tax credit with 2 qualifying children

Fact.Name: Maximum EITC amount with 3 or more children
Fact.Description: The maximum earned income tax credit with 3 or more qualifying children

Fact.Name: Phase in rate for 0 qualifying children
Phase in rate for 1 qualifying child
Phase in rate for 2 qualifying children
Phase in rate for 3 or more qualifying children
EITC phase in rate
Fact.Description: The rate at which EITC phases in with income.

Fact.Name: EITC phase out rate
Fact.Description: The rate at which EITC phases out as income exceeds the threshold amount.

Fact.Name: EITC phaseout amount threshold
Fact.Description: The amount of income above which the amount of EITC begins to be reduced. Rev Proc .06 Earned Income Credit §32(b)

Fact.Name: EITC (MFJ) limit with 3 or more children
Fact.Description: EITC limit with 3 or more children when filing as MFJ Rev Proc .06 Earned Income Credit §32(b)

Fact.Name: EITC (self) limit with 3 or more children
Fact.Description: EITC limit with 3 or more children when not filing as MFJ Rev Proc .06 Earned Income Credit §32(b)

Fact.Name: EITC (MFJ) limit with 2 children
Fact.Description: EITC limit with 2 children when filing as MFJ Rev Proc .06 Earned Income Credit §32(b)

Fact.Name: EITC (self) limit with 2 children
Fact.Description: EITC limit with 2 children when not filing as MFJ Rev Proc .06 Earned Income Credit §32(b)

Fact.Name: EITC (MFJ) limit with 1 child
Fact.Description: EITC limit with 1 child when filing as MFJ Rev Proc .06 Earned Income Credit §32(b)

Fact.Name: EITC (self) limit with 1 child
Fact.Description: EITC limit with 1 child when not filing as MFJ Rev Proc .06 Earned Income Credit §32(b)

Fact.Name: EITC (MFJ) limit with 0 children
Fact.Description: EITC limit with 0 children when filing as MFJ Rev Proc .06 Earned Income Credit §32(b)

Fact.Name: EITC (self) limit with 0 children
Fact.Description: EITC limit with 0 children when not filing as MFJ Rev Proc .06 Earned Income Credit §32(b)

Fact.Name: EITC Completed Phaseout Amount
Fact.Description: The amount of income at which EITC is completely phased out.

Fact.Name: Lower bound phase-out amount
Fact.Description: The lower bound of the midpoint range that is associated with the complete phase-out amount

Fact.Name: Special transition EITC amounts
Fact.Description: The amount EITC given when the user is close to the phaseout amount for their filing status and number of QC

Fact.Name: Whether the TP may be eligible for the EITC
Fact.Description:
Whether the TP passes various tests to be eligible for the Earned Income Tax Credit (EITC) prior to the EITC section of the flow.
**IMPORTANT DIFFERENCE BETWEEN DF AND TWE** We do not include eligibility tests for 1) MFS separatedness; 2) the TP is a QC of another TP; or 3) if the TP has received an improper claims notice in the past

Fact.Name: Whether the TP may be eligible for the EITC Base
Fact.Description: Base rules that define whhether the TP passes various tests to be eligible for the Earned Income Tax Credit (EITC) before running child-specific tests

Fact.Name: TP(s) under 65 age test for EITC with no QC
Fact.Description: TP(s) age test for EITC with no qualifying children if they are less than 65 at the end of the tax year

Fact.Name: TP(s) over 25 age test for EITC with no QC
Fact.Description: TP(s) are eligible EITC with no qualifying children if they are greater or equal to 25 at the end of the tax year

Fact.Name: Whether the TP may be eligible for the EITC
Fact.Description: Whether the TP passes various tests to be eligible for the Earned Income Tax Credit (EITC)

Fact.Name: Whether the TP may be eligible for the EITC
Fact.Description: Whether the TP passes various tests to be eligible for the Earned Income Tax Credit (EITC)

Fact.Name: Below EITC AGI Limit
Fact.Description: Whether the TP's AGI is low enough for the EITC

Fact.Name: Has Earned Income
Fact.Description: Whether the TP had earned income

Fact.Name: Below EITC Earned Income Limit
Fact.Description: Whether the TP's Earned Income is low enough for the EITC.

Fact.Name: Below EITC Investment Income Limit
Fact.Description: Whether the TP's Investment Income is low enough for the EITC Rev Proc .06 Earned Income Credit §32(i)

Fact.Name: Below EITC Investment Income Limit
Fact.Description: Whether the TP's Investment Income is low enough for the EITC Rev Proc .06 Earned Income Credit §32(i)

Fact.Name: The amount of the Earned Income Tax Credit
Fact.Description: The amount of EITC, if the taxpayer is eligible. Magic numbers come from applicable Rev Procs, updated annually. Roughly matches /eitcAmountWithoutCombatPay in other, tax return filing forms
Total deductions as reported on the Form 1040
Itemized Deduction - Standard Deduction. Used for calculating Line 4b net change
OB3 deductions + Standard or Itemized, with coordination from OB3 70111 to remove limitation on top bracket for itemizers

Fact.Name: OB3 Deductions
Fact.Description: Sum of all OB3 below the line deductions

Fact.Name: Wants standard deduction
Fact.Description: Will the taxpayer take the standard deduction?

Fact.Name: Wants itemized deduction
Fact.Description: Will the taxpayer take the itemized deduction?

Whether the standard deduction is used, based on the taxpayer's choice and net benefit.
Effectively, the only time we will *not* use the standard deduction is when the taxpayer wants to itemize, and their itemized deduction amount is greater than the standard deduction amount that they are eligible for.
In all other cases, the standard deduction will be used.
Whether the taxpayer wanted to itemize, but the standard deduction was determined to be of greater benefit

Fact.Name: Standard or itemized deductions
Fact.Description: The taxpayer's choice of standard or itemized deductions as reported on line 12 of Form 1040.
Ignores /amountOfItemizedDeductionsToSubtract when computing /itemizedDeduction as this has a circular dependency on QBI deduction that is explicitly ignored per OB3 70111 when calculation QBID

Fact.Name: Number of CTC-eligible dependents
Fact.Description: The number of dependents who are eligible for the Child Tax Credit. Reported on line 4 of Schedule 8812.

Fact.Name: Number of ODC-eligible dependents
Fact.Description: The number of dependents who are eligible for the Credit for Other Dependents. Reported on line 6 of Schedule 8812.

Fact.Name: Whether the TP may be eligible to receive CTC based on dependents
Fact.Description: Whether there are dependents who are eligible for the Child Tax Credit.

Fact.Name: Whether the TP may be eligible for ODC based on dependents
Fact.Description: Whether there are dependents who are eligible for the Credit for Other Dependents.

Fact.Name: Maximum child tax credit amount
Fact.Description: The maximum child tax credit per child

Fact.Name: Total potential CTC
Fact.Description: The maximum amount of the Child Tax Credit that could be claimed. Reported on line 5 of Schedule 8812.

Fact.Name: Exactly one ODC-eligible dependent
Fact.Description: Whether the taxpayer has exactly one ODC-eligible dependent.

Fact.Name: More than one ODC-eligible dependent
Fact.Description: Whether the taxpayer has more than one ODC-eligible dependent.

Fact.Name: Maximum credit amount for other dependents
Fact.Description: The maximum credit for other dependents, per dependent

Fact.Name: Total potential ODC
Fact.Description: The maximum amount of the Credit for Other Dependents that could be claimed. Reported on line 7 of Schedule 8812.

Fact.Name: Total potential dependent credits
Fact.Description: The combined amount of the maximum Child Tax Credit and Credit for Other Dependents. Reported on line 8 of Schedule 8812.

Fact.Name: Dependent credit phaseout threshold
Fact.Description: The MAGI threshold at which the dependent credits begin to phaseout. Reported on line 9 of Schedule 8812.

Fact.Name: Modified AGI for purposes of dependent credits
Fact.Description: Modified AGI for purposes of dependent credits. Reported on line 3 of Schedule 8812. Schedule 8812 line 2a is 0 because Puerto Rico is out of scope; Schedule 8812 line 2b is 0 because Foreign Earned Income (Form 2555) is out of scope; Schedule 8812 line 2c is 0 because residents of American Samoa (Form 4563) are out of scope; Therefore the MAGI, due to scope, is the AGI

Fact.Name: Dependent credit MAGI above threshold
Fact.Description: The amount of excess income above the threshold.

Fact.Name: Rounded dependent credit MAGI above threshold
Fact.Description: In whole $1,000s, the amount of excess income above the threshold. Per §24(b)(2), any fraction of $1,000 is rounded to $1,000. Reported on line 10 of Form 8812.

Fact.Name: Dependent credit phaseout
Fact.Description: The amount to reduce the dependent credit amount by. Reported on line 11 of Form 8812.

Fact.Name: Dependent credit less phaseout
Fact.Description: The amount of dependent credits left to claim after applying the phaseout. Reported on line 12 of Schedule 8812.

Fact.Name: Dependent credit not fully phased out
Fact.Description: Whether there is any amount of dependent credits left to claim after applying the phaseout. Reported on a checkbox on line 12 of Form 8812.

Fact.Name: Dependent credit fully phased out
Fact.Description: Whether there is no amount of dependent credits left to claim after applying the phaseout. Reported on a checkbox on line 12 of Form 8812.

Fact.Name: Max potential ODC amount after phase out
Fact.Description: The max potential odc amount accounting for phase out if applicable

Fact.Name: Credit Limit Worksheet A Line 4, referencing the output of Credit Limit Worksheet B
Credit Limit for Credits for Qualifying Children and Other Dependents
Fact.Description: The credit limit per Credit Limit Worksheet A Line 5 from Schedule 8812. Reported on line 13.

Fact.Name: Remaining CTC and ODC
Fact.Description: The amount of dependent credits left to claim after crediting the total tentative tax. Reported on line 16a of Form 8812.

Fact.Name: Total potential additional CTC
Fact.Description: The maximum amount of the Additional Child Tax Credit that could be claimed. Reported on line 16b of Schedule 8812.

Fact.Name: Maximum additional child tax credit amount
Fact.Description: The maximum additional tax credit per child Rev Proc .05 Child Tax Credit §24(d)(1)(A)

Fact.Name: Remaining potential additional CTC
Fact.Description: The remaining amount of the Additional Child Tax Credit that could be claimed after crediting the total tentative tax. Schedule 8812 Line 17

Fact.Name: Additional CTC refundability threshold
Fact.Description: The amount of earned income that must be exceeded in order to receive the refundable additional child tax credit.

Fact.Name: Earned income above additional CTC refundability threshold
Fact.Description: The amount of earned income that can be used to calculate the refundable amount of the additional child tax credit. Schedule 8812 Line 19

Fact.Name: Regular maximum additional child tax credit
Fact.Description: The maximum amount of the additional child tax credit that could be refunded to you, absent provisions for taxpayers with three or more children. Schedule 8812 Line 20

Fact.Name: Three or more CTC-eligible dependents
Fact.Description: Whether the taxpayer has three or more CTC-eligible dependents.

Fact.Name: FICA taxes and SE Tax Deduction in excess of EITC claimed
Fact.Description: Schedule 8812 Line 25

Fact.Name: Maximum additional child tax credit
Fact.Description: The maximum amount of the additional child tax credit that could be refunded to you. Schedule 8812 Line 26

Fact.Name: Total CTC from the CTC/ODC
Fact.Description: Total amount of CTC the TP will receive after the phaseout. It is derived and used for us to help the taxpayer make sense of how much of their credit comes from ctc, but it does not exist directly on schedule 8812. CTC credits are applied after ODC credits as per the IRM and when there is a phase out are reduced after ODC credits.

Fact.Name: Total ODC from the CTC/ODC
Fact.Description: Total amount of ODC the TP will receive after the phaseout. It is derived and used for us to help the taxpayer make sense of how much of their credit comes from odc, but it does not exist directly on schedule 8812. ODC credits are applied before CTC credits as per the IRM and is also reduced first if there is a phase out.

Fact.Name: Total CTC and ODC
Fact.Description: The total amount of the child tax credit and credit for other dependents. Reported on line 14 of Form 8812 and line 19 of Form 1040.

Fact.Name: Additional child tax credit
Fact.Description: The refundable amount of the additional child tax credit. Entered on line 27 of Form 8812 and line 28 of Form 1040. Schedule 8812 Line 27

Fact.Name: Maximum non itemizer charitable deduction cap for MFJ filer
Fact.Description: 2000 is maximum a non itemizer can deduct if they are MFJ

Fact.Name: Maximum non itemizer charitable deduction cap for non-MFJ filer
Fact.Description: 1000 is maximum a non itemizer can deduct if they are non-MFJ

Fact.Name: Maximum non itemizer charitable deduction based on filing status
Amount a non-itemizer taxpayer contributed to charitable causes during the tax year

Fact.Description: They are tentatively eligible only if they will take the standard deduction, which we derived by comparing if standard deduction is greater than itemized deduction total

Fact.Name: Non-itemizer charitable deduction
Fact.Description:
OB3 70424, amending IRC 170(p): Special rule for taxpayers who do not elect to itemize deductions. In the case of any taxable year, if the individual does not elect to itemize deductions for such taxable year, the deduction under this section shall be equal to the deduction, not in excess of 1,000 ($2,000 in the case of a joint return), which would be determined under this section if the only charitable contributions taken into account in determining such deduction were contributions made in cash during such taxable year ...
We only expect this deduction to be taken if the filer's standard deduction is greater than their itemized deduction, keeping in line with the logic in "/standardOrItemizedDeduction"
